page: Amazon Web Services m5.16xlarge is a general-purpose server powered by the Nitro hypervisor. It features an Intel Xeon 8175M processor with an x86_64 architecture, providing 64 dedicated vCPUs across 32 physical cores with 2 threads per core. The CPU operates at a clock speed of 3.1 GHz and includes 33 MB of L3 cache. It is equipped with 256.0 GB of DDR4 memory running at 3 GHz, yielding 4.0 GB of RAM per core. This instance does not include local storage or GPU accelerators, and it offers a baseline network bandwidth of 20 Gbps.

Performance benchmarks indicate a clear contrast between single-core and multi-core capabilities. Single-core performance is relatively weak, as evidenced by lower scores in stress-ng single-core, geekbench single-core, and single-threaded compression tests. However, multi-core and memory bandwidth metrics are significantly stronger. Large-block memory bandwidth read and write operations rank in the top tier, supported by strong performance in extended CPU instruction tests. Multi-threaded decompression, software compilation, and database operations perform at average levels, making the instance highly capable for parallelized workloads.

Given its balanced 1:4 vCPU-to-RAM ratio and strong multi-core memory bandwidth, the m5.16xlarge is qualitatively cost-efficient for resource-dense, multi-threaded applications that do not require local NVMe storage or GPU acceleration. It is well-suited for workloads such as static web serving, multi-threaded software compilation, database operations, and medium-scale LLM prompt processing. However, it is less optimal for single-threaded tasks or real-time text generation where single-core latency is a critical bottleneck.
description: Amazon Web Services m5.16xlarge is a general-purpose server configured with 64 dedicated vCPUs and 256.0 GB of DDR4 memory, running on an Intel Xeon 8175M processor at 3.1 GHz. Built on the Nitro hypervisor, this x86_64 instance provides a balanced 1:4 vCPU-to-RAM ratio but lacks local storage and GPU hardware. The processor features 33 MB of L3 cache and supports AVX-512 instructions. Benchmark data shows weak single-core performance, but strong large-block memory read and write bandwidth alongside average multi-core execution. This makes it a qualitatively cost-efficient option for parallelized workloads. It is best suited for multi-threaded applications, database operations, static web serving, and software compilation, while being less optimal for single-threaded tasks.
og_description: Amazon Web Services m5.16xlarge is a general-purpose server featuring 64 dedicated vCPUs, 256 GB DDR4 RAM, and 20 Gbps network bandwidth. It is optimized for multi-threaded workloads and database operations.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services m5.16xlarge is a general-purpose server featuring 64 dedicated vCPUs, 256 GB DDR4 RAM, and 20 Gbps baseline network bandwidth.
tagline: A balanced general-purpose server featuring dedicated multi-core processing and high-bandwidth memory for parallelized enterprise workloads.
bullet_points: 64 dedicated vCPUs and 256 GB DDR4 memory on Intel Xeon 8175M architecture
Strong large-block memory read and write bandwidth performance
20 Gbps baseline network bandwidth with Nitro hypervisor integration
No local storage or GPU accelerators included in the configuration
Best suited for multi-threaded applications, database operations, and web serving
